steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option from the dropdown menu under "New Project" to create an agent by declaring instructions, actions, and knowledge.

Click on the dropdown option "General Teams Agent" under "Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK." This option provides functionality for a Teams agent that can chat with users in Teams via the Teams AI Library and connect to LLMs.
Click on the "Simple Bot" option in the dropdown menu under the "Teams Capability" section to select a simple implementation of a web app ready for customization.
Click on the "TypeScript" option within the dropdown menu labeled "Programming Language" in the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface.
Click on the "Default Folder" input field within the "Workspace Folder" dialog to specify the project location.
Click the "Dashboard" option in the left sidebar menu of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Press the "End" key to navigate to the bottom of the "Dashboard" section within the Developer Portal interface.
Press "End" key to scroll to the bottom of the "Dashboard" page in the Developer Portal interface.
Click the "Sign in to your account" tab in the browser navigation bar.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page to submit the password.
Click on the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the dropdown menu within the "New Project" dialog of Visual Studio Code, specifically under the context of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu labeled "Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK" within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Simple Bot" option from the dropdown menu in the Microsoft Teams Toolkit interface within Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "TypeScript" option in the programming language dropdown menu located within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown option in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the folder path (`/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`).
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the text input in the "Application Name" field within the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion 'TDP' is exist
Click the "Add" button in the app details pop-up for the "tdp_app_HW4km" within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Open" button on the popup dialog titled "Added successfully!" to launch and start using the newly added app (tdp_app_HW4km) within the Microsoft Teams interface.
@assertion ${{var:app_name}} is exist
